So when you log into apartment IQ, the first thing, the first place you're gonna be taken to upon opening a market survey is this property tab, the way we navigate through a market survey.
And apartment IQ is these four tabs at the top.

And as we move from left to right through the unit type, the units and ultimately the recommendations tab, we're kind of going from the area of highest uh insights or the the highest level insights down through more levels of granularity until we get to specific unit evaluations from this first page.
The first thing you'll probably notice is this 12 week trailing historical graph.
This is going to have for you by default, the new available units that were added every week over the last 12 weeks.
So that immediately you have an understanding visually of where the supply has been in your market.
On the right side, you'll have a leader board and this leader board is going to show us exactly who's doing the most activity depending on what the filter is here.
So let's say we have this set to available units.
What we can see here is that there were eight units added this week and these uh uh three properties have added the most.

We know what's the supply has been, but what's the leasing activity been like we have the same 12 weeks, but now we're looking at units that have been coming off websites as leased.
And again, we have our leader board here at the top right, our podium as we sometimes like to call it, which shows the top five properties by activity on their leasing.

So what's nice about this is when we juxtapose these two together, we can kind of get an understanding of where our market is and what that might mean for our marketing and pricing initiatives going forward and how we think about that

In this table?

at the bottom of each table in apartment IQ, you'll find averages and totals of every single one of these particular metrics so that you can always measure up against the average benchmark for the area.
